title: pastinaca pratensis
intro: Pastinaca pratensis, also known as wild parsnip, is a biennial plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to Europe and Asia. The plant contains furanocoumarins, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. Wild parsnip has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve pain, and reduce inflammation. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as used in topical applications, to harness its medicinal properties.
